item.text: The finding points at operation validation in `src/scimPatch.ts`. There’s no git metadata under `/work`, so I’ll avoid any version-control assumptions and work directly against the source.

item.text: I’ve confirmed validation only checks the operation name and path type. The pollution risk likely comes from accepting dangerous path segments or object keys that are later assigned into user/resource objects, so I’m tracing every write site before editing.

The write sites are all dynamic property writes: direct path writes, nested assignment from object-valued patches, and array-filter creation for missing values. I’m going to add a shared guard for path segments and parsed filter attributes, then cover the known pollution vectors with tests.

item.text: I’m making the guard central: validation will reject dangerous path segments before navigation, and the lower-level assignment helper will also reject them so object-valued patches cannot bypass validation through dotted keys.

The first code change blocks `__proto__`, `prototype`, and `constructor` as SCIM attribute path segments and avoids walking inherited properties during nested assignment. I noticed one synthesized-object branch uses a parsed filter attribute as a key, so I’m guarding that too.

Next I’m adding regression tests at the validation and patch-application layers: one for direct malicious paths, one for dotted keys inside object-valued patches, and one for the missing-array synthesized object path.
I’m placing the runtime regression tests in the existing `invalid requests` group so they exercise the same public `scimPatch` entry point used by consumers.
The remediation and regression tests are in place. I’m installing from the existing lockfile now so I can run the TypeScript build and test suite locally.

The TypeScript build and lint pass. I’m running the full test suite now, including the new pollution regressions.

item.text: The full mocha suite passes with 110 tests. I’m doing one final source diff-style inspection so I can summarize exactly what changed and make sure no generated artifacts need attention.

item.text:
Implemented the prototype pollution remediation.

Changed:
- [src/scimPatch.ts](/work/src/scimPatch.ts:68): blocks `__proto__`, `prototype`, and `constructor` as patch path/object-value keys.
- [src/scimPatch.ts](/work/src/scimPatch.ts:445): nested assignment now validates key paths and avoids walking inherited properties.
- [test/patchValidation.test.ts](/work/test/patchValidation.test.ts:80) and [test/scimPatch.test.ts](/work/test/scimPatch.test.ts:1235): added regression coverage for direct paths, object-valued dotted keys, and synthesized array-filter objects.

Verification passed:
- `npm run build`
- `npm run lint`
- `npm run mocha` → `110 passing`
